behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is the local grouping rule (keying by trace_id, not service), which can fail inside one function. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: behavior text only requires correct grouping semantics, not cross-module wiring or real infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the grouping function with mixed-service JSON records sharing/differing trace IDs; assert groups are keyed only by trace ID and contain entries from multiple services when IDs match.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is timestamp-ordering/skew-tolerance logic. MOCK-MISS: a pure algorithm test can expose wrong skew handling. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration: behavior does not require proving module handoff, only ordering correctness under a defined skew bound.
behaviors.test_description: Feed synthetic events from multiple services into the ordering/skew-normalization routine with known ±500ms skew cases; assert final timeline order matches expected causal order and boundary handling at exactly 500ms.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: Behavior includes both detection and report flagging, so failure can occur at module handoff (metrics calculator -> anomaly detector -> report builder). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow due deterministic in-process analytics and reporting.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process pipeline test wiring baseline calculator, p99 detector, and anomaly report formatter; input baseline window plus spike window where p99 exceeds 3x; assert anomaly report contains the spike flag with correct metric context.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode is resilience + accounting across parser, pipeline control flow, and reporting counter; this is module interaction, not only parse-function correctness. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; no AI dependence; no multi-agentic sequence. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real filesystem/socket ingestion.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the in-process ingest pipeline with a mixed stream of valid and malformed JSON records; assert pipeline completes, valid records are processed, malformed records are skipped, and reported skipped-count equals malformed input count.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE: burst throughput/drop/lag failures are often due to runtime scheduling, buffering, backpressure, and process-level behavior that in-process fakes can hide. Requires real process execution under load. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration: behavior explicitly targets high-rate runtime characteristics, not just logical wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual tool process in a load test harness that sends 10,000 structured entries/sec for a fixed interval (e.g., via stdin/socket used in production). Measure ingested vs emitted counts and processing lag; assert zero drops and lag remains within configured non-falling-behind threshold.
